stories.headline: EU foreign ministers reject proposal to suspend association agreement with Israel
stories.summary: A part suspension was tabled by Ireland, Spain and Slovenia but did not receive enough backing from other member statesThe EU remains split on imposing sanctions on Israel, despite some member states criticising the country over the plight of Gaza and violence against Palestinians by Israeli settlers in the West Bank.Kaja Kallas, the EU foreign policy chief, said proposals for a part suspension of the EU-Israel association agreement remained on t

stories.headline: Zelenskyy announces Druzhba oil pipeline repaired and 'can resume operation'
stories.summary: The repair of the Druzhba pipeline paves the way for the adoption of the €90 billion loan for Ukraine and a new round of sanctions against Russia, both of which are currently vetoed by Hungary.

stories.headline: Two Palestinians killed during settler attack on West Bank village, officials say
stories.summary: A 14-year-old boy was among those shot dead, according to the mayor of al-Mughayyir.

stories.headline: Decision on EU’s €90bn loan for Ukraine ‘due in next 24 hours’ after Zelenskyy says oil pipeline repaired – as it happened
stories.summary: EU’s foreign policy chief, Kaja Kallas, says there is ‘new momentum’ after Hungarian election as Ukrainian leader says Druzhba pipeline can resume operationsGerman foreign minister Joseph Wadephul also makes it very clear that he is relieved with the change of government in Hungary, calling it “a breath of fresh air” and a promise of hope for Ukraine.He urged Hungary to drop its “unusual blockade” for policies for Ukraine “as quickly as possible,

stories.headline: Ukraine says Druzhba pipeline running Russian oil to Europe can resume work
stories.summary: Kyiv hopeful pipeline restoration will unblock EU loan, with bloc's foreign policy chief expecting 'positive decision'.

stories.headline: Why is the EU under pressure to suspend its trade agreement with Israel?
stories.summary: Divided EU nations debate suspending Israel deal as pressure mounts over rights violations in Gaza and the West Bank.

stories.headline: Israeli soldiers and settlers kill 11 Palestinians across Gaza, West Bank

stories.summary: Iran has cast doubt on negotiations and says US has violated the truce

stories.headline: Trump says he opposes extending Iran ceasefire amid talks uncertainty
stories.summary: US president says Iran has 'no choice' but to show up to the negotiations in Pakistan and accept a 'great' deal.

stories.headline: Rabbi accused of war crimes selected for Israel’s national celebration
stories.summary: Rabbi Avraham Zarbiv has filmed himself demolishing Palestinian buildings in Gaza while reciting religious verses.

stories.headline: Israeli army jails two soldiers for smashing head of Jesus statue in southern Lebanon
stories.summary: Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu had said on Monday he was "stunned and saddened to learn that an IDF soldier damaged a Catholic religious icon in southern Lebanon."

stories.headline: UK ambassador to UAE warns of regional risk but backs UAE resilience amid fragile Iran ceasefire
stories.summary: In an interview with Euronews, British Ambassador to the United Arab Emirates Edward Hobart said the fragile US-Iran ceasefire should pave the way to a lasting peace with "a clear way forward".

stories.headline: EU ministers reject calls to suspend Israeli trade agreement over 'war crimes'
stories.summary: Spain, Ireland and Slovenia’s proposal to suspend the EU–Israel Association Agreement over alleged war crimes in Lebanon and Palestine failed to secure sufficient support among EU foreign ministers. However, a separate proposal concerning settlement goods will be referred to the European Commission.

stories.headline: Qatar backs ceasefire as uncertainty surrounds US-Iran peace talks in Islamabad
stories.summary: Qatar has urged diplomacy on Tuesday as uncertainty surrounds US-Iran talks, warning prolonged tensions risk wider global fallout, particularly if disruption in the Strait of Hormuz affects energy flows and markets.

stories.headline: Palestine weekly wrap: Coordinated attacks and evictions in Gaza, West Bank
stories.summary: The killings of Palestinians in Gaza and settler violence across the West Bank are both continuing to surge.

stories.headline: Shot by Israeli forces, Hala, 18 and Mohammad, 13 fight for their lives
stories.summary: A Gaza bride-to-be and a 13-year-old West Bank boy fight for their lives after being shot in the head by Israeli forces.

stories.headline: Emerging FX Wavers as War Jitters Mount Ahead of Truce Deadline
stories.summary: An index for emerging-market dropped, reversing earlier gains, amid uncertainty over whether Vice President JD Vance will travel to Islamabad for peace talks as the clock ticks down to the ceasefire deadline on Wednesday.

stories.headline: Vance’s Trip to Islamabad on Hold as U.S. Says Iran Failed to Respond to Its Terms
stories.summary: The delay is another hurdle in the Trump administration’s push to secure an agreement that would curb Iran’s nuclear program.

stories.headline: What to Know About Virginia’s Redistricting Fight
stories.summary: The election on Tuesday is part of a coast-to-coast fight between Republicans and Democrats ahead of the midterms.

stories.summary: As the United States and Iran make a second attempt at a deal, their negotiating styles are on a collision course.

stories.headline: What do we know about the ABINSK ship, and why wasn't it stopped?
stories.summary: The ship recently docked at an Israeli port and is en route to Turkey despite allegations it is carrying wheat from Russian-occupied Ukraine.

stories.headline: Japan on high alert for 'huge' second quake after lifting tsunami warning
stories.summary: Japan's meteorological agency warns another, stronger earthquake may hit in the next week.

stories.headline: Warsh signals evolution, not revolution at the Fed
stories.summary: The nominee for chair of the central bank wants regime change, but at a slow and deliberative pace

stories.headline: Warsh Says Fed ‘Must Stay in Its Lane’
stories.summary: Bloomberg's Mike McKee detailed changes to the Federal Reserve that fed chair nominee Kevin Warsh revealed in his confirmation hearing Tuesday, saying that Warsh may want to change or eliminate economic guidance. Warsh told Senators that he is not a fan of the Fed's forward guidance and feels that it sticks to it too closely. Warsh also indicated that he would aim to hem in federal reserve members speaking out ahead of rate decisions.  (Source: B

stories.headline: Warsh Pledges Independence But Dodges Questions on Rates
stories.summary: Kevin Warsh repeatedly pledged to act independently if he’s confirmed as the next Federal Reserve chair, rejecting Democratic concerns he would be a “sock puppet” for President Donald Trump, who continues to demand lower interest rates. (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: Treasuries Weaken Before Warsh Testifies for Fed Chair Role
stories.summary: Treasuries fell as strong economic data and higher oil prices prompted traders to scale back rate-cut expectations, overshadowing testimony from Federal Reserve Chair nominee Kevin Warsh.

stories.headline: Tim Cook's Exit, Warsh Faces Congress | Open Interest 4/21/2026
stories.summary: Get a jump start on the US trading day with Matt Miller and Dani Burger on "Bloomberg Open Interest." Apple’s era shifts as Tim Cook steps down after 15 years, with John Ternus set to take over.  And Kevin Warsh faces Capitol Hill in a high-stakes Fed showdown. We'll take you to Capitol Hill. Plus, UnitedHealth rebounds with higher premiums despite shrinking enrollment, and airlines juggle strong demand against rising costs—how long can tra

stories.headline: Here Are the Key Takeaways From Kevin Warsh’s Fed Confirmation Hearing
stories.summary: Here are the key takeaways from Kevin Warsh’s remarks at his confirmation hearing to be the next chairman of the Federal Reserve. He testified before the US Senate Banking Committee on Tuesday.

stories.headline: Sheila Cherfilus-McCormick, a Florida Democrat, Resigns From Congress
stories.summary: Sheila Cherfilus-McCormick stands accused of stealing $5 million in federal funds. She announced she would step down minutes before the House Ethics Committee was to vote on a recommendation to expel her.

stories.headline: Ohio State Details Relationship that Led to Former President Walter Carter Jr.’s Resignation
stories.summary: The university released a report that said the president, Walter Carter Jr., took trips with a podcaster and suggested that the university hire her.

stories.headline: The Redistricting War Has Come to Virginia
stories.summary: Voters in Virginia are deciding whether to adopt a new gerrymandered congressional map that benefits Democrats. Nick Corasaniti, a politics reporter, explains the role Virginia’s new map is playing in the nationwide redistricting battle and its potential implications for the midterm elections.

stories.headline: House Democrat resigns ahead of possible expulsion vote over alleged financial crimes
stories.summary: Rep. Sheila Cherfilus-McCormick was indicted last year on charges that she stole and laundered federal disaster money and used it to support a congressional campaign.

stories.headline: EU to boost jet fuel imports from the United States amid shortage fears
stories.summary: EU Transport Commissioner Apostolos Tzitzikostas has said there are no indications of jet fuel shortages, but stressed the importance of acting quickly and appropriately. The use of emergency stocks is under consideration "if real supply issues arise".

stories.headline: Unprecedented ruling finds Hungary's anti-LGBTQ laws in breach of EU values
stories.summary: The EU's top court finds that the reforms breached EU values on a number of levels and broke the founding values of the EU treaty.

stories.headline: Japan loosens arms export rules in break from post-WW2 pacifism

stories.summary: Strategy pivot coincides with slowdown and fierce competition in world’s largest automotive market

stories.summary: Comprehensive cross-platform coverage of the U.S. market close on Bloomberg Television, Bloomberg Radio, and YouTube with Romaine Bostick, Isabelle Lee, Carol Massar and Tim Stenovec. (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.summary: Core Scientific Inc. is looking to raise $3.3 billion from a junk-bond sale, joining a wave of high-yield issuers tapping debt markets to fund artificial intelligence infrastructure.

stories.headline: Taiwan president blames China for forced cancellation of Eswatini trip
stories.summary: Lai Ching-te abandons visit after Seychelles, Mauritius and Madagascar revoke overflight permission  Taiwan’s president, Lai Ching-te, has cancelled his trip to Eswatini, the democratic island’s only diplomatic ally in Africa, after his government said several countries had revoked overflight permits because of “intense pressure” from China.Lai was to leave on Wednesday for the 40th anniversary of King Mswati III’s accession. stories.headline: Sam Neill says New Zealand goldmine supporters have threatened him with violence
stories.summary: Actor, who has publicly objected to plans to fast-track project near his farm, says he has received personal abuseThe actor Sam Neill says he has received threats of violence from supporters of a controversial goldmine that could be opened several kilometres away from his farm in New Zealand’s Central Otago district, after he publicly objected to the government’s plans to fast-track the mine.The Australian mining company Santana Minerals is pushi

stories.headline: Hot air balloon makes emergency landing in California backyard
stories.summary: "Look at that, wow!" - A couple in Temecula woke up to find a balloon boarded with 13 people behind their home on Saturday.

stories.headline: Trump says US will not lift Hormuz blockade until deal made with Iran

stories.headline: US Stock ETF Rises Late as Trump Extends Ceasefire: Markets Wrap
stories.summary: A giant exchange-traded fund tracking US stocks climbed in late hours after President Donald Trump said he’s extending the ceasefire deal with Iran until talks conclude.

stories.headline: Oil Rises Above $100 as US, Iran Peace Talks Uncertain
stories.summary: Oil prices rose amid reports that the US and Iran will put on hold talks to secure a peace deal before a ceasefire expires on Wednesday, dampening hopes that flows through the critical Strait of Hormuz will resume in the near future.

stories.headline: Gold Falls Most in Two Weeks as US-Iran Talks Hang in Balance
stories.summary: Gold declined the most in more than two weeks as traders awaited confirmation that Iran will join the US in peace talks before a ceasefire expires, with the two sides still at odds on key issues.

stories.headline: Australia's most-decorated soldier vows to fight war crime charges
stories.summary: Ben Roberts-Smith has given his first statement since he was charged with five counts of the war crime of murder last week.

stories.headline: India has splurged billions on metro trains. But where are the commuters?
stories.summary: Without better last-mile connectivity and affordable fares, metro use is unlikely to improve quickly, say experts.

stories.headline: Art on trial - a sculptor's arrest highlights new extremes for censorship in China
stories.summary: Gao Zhen is being retroactively punished for 15 year-old-works, in a case that has alarmed rights groups.

stories.headline: Drone footage shows huge Malaysian coastal village fire
stories.summary: Thousands of people have been displaced after a fire destroyed around 1,000 homes in Malaysia's Sabah state.

stories.headline: Watch: Runners v robots at China half marathon

stories.headline: Chernobyl's last wedding: The couple who married as a nuclear disaster unfolded
stories.summary: Serhiy and Iryna married 40 years ago, unaware a nuclear reactor had exploded less than 3 miles away.
